1 Introduction to Positron vs. RStudio

1.1 Essay Prompt 1:

Based on what you learned from Step 1 and Step 3, what do you like about Positron compared with RStudio?

NoteLearning Reflection

Positron has many flexible features and combines the best parts of VS Code with prohramming languages used in data science that works well.

Based on the Step 1 and Step 3 videos, here is what I liked about Positron compared to RStudio:

  • Better Multi-Language Support: As RStudio was mostly made for R, Positron treats both R and Python the same. You can easily switch between the two in the same workspace without any issues.

  • Modern and Fast: Since Positron is built on the same framework as VS Code, that means it is faster, more lightweight, and it allows you to use your preferred VS Code themes, shortcuts, and extensions.

  • Smoother Data Viewing: The upgraded Data Explorer is much improved compared to RStudio’s old View() function. This data explorer allows you to sort, filter, and analyze your data in a clean and interactive panel that won’t lag or freeze on larger datasets.

2 Artificial Intelligence in Positron

2.1 Essay Prompt 2:

Describe the various ways you can use AI inside Positron. Some are free while others are not.

Positron is built like VS Code which means it works perfectly with modern AI coding tools. Here are three main ways you can use AI in the app:

  • Github Copilot
  • VS Code AI Extensions (Continue.dev / Codeium / Tabnine)
  • Inline AI Commands
  • Type: Paid subscription (but free for verified students and teachers).
  • Features: Suggests code right in your editor as you type. It can predict whole blocks of code, build data pipelines such as Tidyverse or Pandas, and it can write code comments for you.
  • Type: Mix of free tiers and paid upgrades.
  • Features: It adds a chat sidebar and you can highlight any code in your file. You can ask AI to rewrite it, find bugs, or explain how a function works.
  • Type: Tool-dependent.
  • Features: Allows a quick shortcut such as Ctrl+I or Cmd+I to open a small prompt window right where you are currently typing. You can also ask for code without having to open a sidebar.

2.2 Essay Prompt 3:

Which AI tools have you installed or set up? Which AI tools did you find beneficial for you?

I successfully set up GitHub Copilot which as been helpful in terms of working with Quarto (.qmd) files while automatically guessing my next dplyr data steps before I begin typing them.

2.3 Essay Prompt 4: GitHub Copilot Experience & Screenshot

2.3.1 GitHub Education Status

Below is the verification of my acceptance into the GitHub Education program, enabling free access to GitHub Copilot:

2.3.2 Personal Evaluation: Helpful vs. Distracting

  • Why it is helpful: Memorizing minor syntax details such as specific ggplot2 configurations aren’t needed since it functions as a highly efficient autocomplete engine. It speeds up writing repetitive code which is helpful.

  • When it can be Distracting: Occasionally, it will suggest incorrect or outdated functions. However, accepting the suggestions without checking can cause bugs that take longer to fix than writing the code manually.

  • Conclusion: Overall, I believe it is a highly effective tool that assists you rather than distracts. This is true as long as the final code is carefully reviewed and in control.
